question_text: Let 𝑓 be a continuous function from [0, 1] to the set of all real numbers. Then which one of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: For any sequence {𝑥𝑛}𝑛≥1 in [0, 1], ∑ 𝑓(𝑥𝑛) 𝑛=1 is absolutely convergent
options.C: If {𝑥𝑛}𝑛≥1 is a sequence in [0, 1] such that {𝑓(𝑥𝑛)}𝑛≥1 is convergent, then {𝑥𝑛}𝑛≥1 is convergent
options.D: If 𝑓 is also monotonically increasing, then the image of 𝑓 is given by [𝑓(0), 𝑓(1)]
